description: Knox surfaced Speaker 3 and Speaker 4 on the May 5 recording with sample_text of 'I understand,' and 'Oh shit. Was that the junior guy?' respectively. These were the raw sample_text values from the _speakers.json file -- far too short to identify a speaker. Knox should have pulled the first 5 utterances from the .md transcript for each speaker before presenting to the controller. Instead, David had to explicitly request 'Give me segments for speaker 3 & 4 so I can determine who is who.'
correction: Knox read the full .md transcript and extracted representative utterance samples. David then confirmed Speaker 3 = Megan Wehrle (spoke most), Speaker 4 = Beau Wehrle.
systemic_fix: Edge Case E (sample text too short) is now documented in both skills/plaud-transcripts/SKILL.md (section 4c) and step-03-identify-speakers.md (EDGE CASE CHECKS, section E). Rule: if sample_text is 5 words or fewer, do NOT present it to the controller. Instead, read the .md transcript and extract the first 5 utterances attributed to that speaker label. Present those instead. This check must run before surfacing ANY pending speaker to the controller.
